commits.message:
mit-styling-aj1.1: hero masthead — the light engraved cover plate

Give the Hero masthead its own signature: the page's one LIGHT, engraved
masthead — Fraunces struck in ink on bone, the inverse of the dark landing
words below, so it reads as the cover before the cinematic descent.

- Spend Fraunces' optical axis (--type-engrave) on the headline so it reads
  cut-in-stone, not body text enlarged. Authored statically, not animated:
  opsz changes glyph advance-width, so an ambient breath would reflow the
  headline between one and two lines each cycle. [LAW:no-ambient-temporal-coupling]
- The italic accent wears IRIS — the colour story's 'surface' family — sourced
  from --acc-iris instead of a twin hex, and inherits the headline's opsz so
  the engraving is one fact across the masthead. [LAW:one-source-of-truth]
- The lone bobbing arrow becomes a scroll-axis: a hairline descender dropping
  into the page, the first inch of the descent.

Whole signature lives in the .hero CSS block; the shared paint loop is
untouched. Verified in Chrome (desktop + 390px mobile): zero console
errors/warnings, no reflow, duplicates no other section's painter.

mit-cut-plan-lsh.4: scene typography — one scale that spends Fraunces' optical axis (#13)

Scene text was ~40 independent clamp() calls; two scenes at the same
typographic role (a frame-foot readout, a landing word, a code line) disagreed
only by incidental ±2px drift. Author the scale once in :root and reference a
named step at every callsite — the fourth facet of the cut-plan epic, in the
same house pattern as motion (lsh.1), color (lsh.2) and pacing (lsh.3).

Unlike those three, type is read by no JS — every size is consumed by CSS
layout — so there is no cssNum() mirror to keep; the single source is simply
:root, referenced through var().

- Ten named steps across three voices: Fraunces serif (masthead → caption),
  Inter sans (readout, label, micro), and a monospace machine voice (mono).
- The big landing moments (masthead → phrase) spend Fraunces' optical-size axis
  deliberately at its display extreme (opsz 144) via --type-engrave, so they
  read as engraving rather than body text enlarged. font-weight is preserved
  (the axis sets opsz only). The smaller serif tiers keep auto optical sizing,
  where legibility — not grandeur — is the job.
- The page-chrome titles (.hero, .epilogue) keep their vw-based clamps: they are
  document-level, not container-scoped scenes.

[LAW:one-source-of-truth] every scene size lives once, in :root.
[LAW:one-type-per-behavior] same typographic role → same step, one value.
[FRAMING:representation] the optical axis is now authored intent, not a side
effect of size.

Verified in Chrome: all 41 scene callsites resolve to a scale step; opsz 144 is
live on the landing tiers with weight intact; Fraunces loaded; no legibility
loss at desktop (V.2) or 360px (T.2) across readout/micro/mono/label and the
weight-400 scaleword; no console errors.
